equations.formula: E = K × f² × A × I / r (differential-mode loop radiation)
equations.verification_source: MSU EMC Lab Module 9 p.9-16: |E|=1.317e-14×f²×A×I/r (matches code 1.316e-14). Derived from Paul 'Introduction to EMC' (Wiley, 1992). URL: https://www.egr.msu.edu/emrg/sites/default/files/content/module9_radiated_emissions.pdf [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-P12-refactor: formula E=K×f²×A×I/r with K=1.316e-14 (free-space) or 2.632e-14 (with ground plane image) is unchanged; hash drift from type-hint additions and code-comment cleanup only.]

Standard dB conversion: dBuV/m = 20×log10(V/m × 1e6). Self-evident from dB definition. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

equations.formula: E = 1.257e-6 × f × L × I_CM / r (common-mode cable radiation)
equations.verification_source: MSU EMC Lab Module 9 p.9-20: |E|=1.257e-6×f×L×I_C/r. Derived from Paul 'Introduction to EMC' (Wiley, 1992) p.415. URL: https://www.egr.msu.edu/emrg/sites/default/files/content/module9_radiated_emissions.pdf [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-P12-refactor: formula E=1.257e-6×f×L×I_CM/r unchanged; coefficient 1.257e-6=µ₀ includes ×2 ground-plane image factor; hash drift from type-hint additions only.]

LearnEMC trapezoid harmonics calculator confirms sinc×sinc envelope structure. URL: https://learnemc.com/ext/calculators/trapezoid_harmonics/index.html. Also MSU Module 9 p.9-17 Figure 7. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-P12-refactor: formula A_n=|a₀×sinc(nπτ/T)×sinc(nπt_r/T)| unchanged; a₀=2×V_peak×τ/T; hash drift from type-hint additions and docstring expansion only.]

MSU Module 9 p.9-17: corner frequencies at 1/(πτ) and 1/(πτ_r) confirmed in emission spectrum plot. URL: https://www.egr.msu.edu/emrg/sites/default/files/content/module9_radiated_emissions.pdf [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-P12-refactor: formula f₁=1/(πτ), f₂=1/(πt_r) unchanged; hash drift from type-hint additions and Tuple return annotation only.]

LearnEMC cavity resonance calculator: f_mn = c/(2√εr)×√((m/a)²+(n/b)²). URL: https://learnemc.com/ext/calculators/cavity_resonance/pcb-res.html. Standard rectangular waveguide cavity formula. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-P12-refactor: formula f_mn=(c/2√εr)×√((m/L)²+(n/W)²) unchanged; uses C_0 constant and math.sqrt; hash drift from type-hint additions and docstring update only.]

Bogatin Rules of Thumb #1: BW=0.35/RT. URL: https://www.edn.com/rule-of-thumb-1-bandwidth-of-a-signal-from-its-rise-time/. Also Signal Integrity Journal: https://www.signalintegrityjournal.com/blogs/12-fundamentals/post/853-back-to-basics-bandwidth-and-rise-time [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

Paul 'Introduction to EMC' Ch. 7: f_knee=0.5/t_r (more conservative than BW=0.35/t_r). Widely cited in EMC literature. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-P12-refactor: formula f_knee=0.5/rise_time_s unchanged; hash drift from type-hint addition (rise_time_s: float -> float) only.]
Standard EM wave propagation: λ=c/(f√εr). Self-evident from Maxwell equations. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

sources: Goldfarb & Pucel, IEEE MGWL Vol.1 No.6 pp.135-137, June 1991
equations.verification_source: Goldfarb & Pucel, IEEE MGWL Vol.1 No.6, June 1991, pp.135-137. Semantic Scholar: https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Modeling-via-hole-grounds-in-microstrip-Goldfarb-Pucel/a3f4614877b750e7b7eec1dfa5924d5ce8b99301. Formula: L≈0.2h(ln(4h/d)+1) nH. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

equations.formula: C = ε₀εr/d (interplane capacitance per unit area)
equations.verification_source: Parallel-plate capacitor: C=ε₀εr/d. Self-evident from Gauss law. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

Series LC resonance: f=1/(2π√(LC)). Self-evident from circuit theory. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-P12-refactor: formula f_SRF=1/(2π√(ESL×C)) unchanged; implementation uses 2*math.pi*math.sqrt(esl_h*capacitance_f); hash drift from type-hint additions and guard-clause reorder only.]
Series RLC impedance: |Z|=√(R²+(XL-XC)²). Self-evident from circuit theory. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]
Conductor resistance: R=ρL/(W×T). Self-evident from Ohm law + conductor geometry. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

Annular ring resistance from via plating geometry. Derived from R=ρH/A where A=π(r²outer-r²inner). [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

sources: Goldfarb & Pucel, IEEE MGWL Vol.1 No.6 pp.135-137, June 1991
equations.verification_source: Goldfarb & Pucel, IEEE MGWL Vol.1 No.6, 1991. Same as EQ-010. URL: https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Modeling-via-hole-grounds-in-microstrip-Goldfarb-Pucel/a3f4614877b750e7b7eec1dfa5924d5ce8b99301 [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

equations.formula: C = ε₀εrLT/S (inter-trace coupling capacitance, parallel-plate approx)
equations.verification_source: Parallel-plate capacitor approximation for coupling. Marked as rough estimate in source code. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]
RC filter cutoff: f_c=1/(2πRC). Self-evident from circuit theory. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]
LC resonance: f₀=1/(2π√(LC)). Self-evident from circuit theory. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]
LC characteristic impedance: Z₀=√(L/C). Self-evident from circuit theory. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

Wheeler narrow microstrip: Z₀=(60/√εr)ln(8h/w+w/4h) for w/h<1. Confirmed at: https://f4inx.github.io/posts/microstrip-formulas-comparison.html. IPC-2141 standard. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

Wheeler wide microstrip: Z₀=120π/(√εr(w/h+1.393+0.667ln(w/h+1.444))) for w/h≥1. Confirmed at: https://f4inx.github.io/posts/microstrip-formulas-comparison.html. IPC-2141 standard. [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]

equations.notes: Source corrected: originally cited 'Power Distribution Network Design Methodologies' which is by Novák (IPC, 2008), not Smith. Smith's PDN work is in 'Principles of Power Integrity for PDN Design' (Prentice Hall, 2017) with Bogatin.

Ott 'EMC Engineering' (Wiley, 2009) Section 7.3; LearnEMC trapezoid harmonics calculator [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-P12-refactor: function estimate_switching_emissions uses trapezoidal_corner_frequencies (EQ-005) and trapezoidal_harmonic_amplitude (EQ-004) for envelope calculation; the docstring simplified rectangular form V_n=V_peak×2/(nπ)×sin(nπD) is the theoretical basis, actual computation delegates to EQ-004/005; SPICE FFT branch added but analytical path unchanged; hash drift from SPICE backend integration and output dict expansion only.]
equations.notes: This is the simplified rectangular (zero rise-time) form used as the harmonic envelope. The full trapezoidal form with rise/fall time is EQ-004 which uses sinc×sinc.

equations.formula: A_track = Σ(width_mm × length_mm) (switching node copper area)
sources: Engineering heuristic — switching node area directly
equations.verification_source: TI AN-2155 (SNVA638A): minimize SW node copper area; ADI AN-139: switching node antenna effect [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]
equations.notes: Not a physics equation per se — it is a geometric area calculation used as a proxy for antenna radiation potential. The underlying principle (larger copper area at high dV/dt = more radiation) is well-established in industry app notes.

sources: Novak "Power Distribution Network Design Methodologies" (IPC, 2008) Ch. 6
equations.verification_source: Re-verified after refactoring (AnalysisContext migration, design_rules split) - formula unchanged, only surrounding code moved [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; formula unchanged, hash drift from type hints / docstring updates.]
equations.notes: Source corrected: originally cited 'Power Distribution Network Design Methodologies Ch. 6' which is by Novák. The actual implementation is more sophisticated than simple P/V — it models topology-dependent AC current components (buck: I_peak*(1-D), boost: 30% of I_in_avg).

equations.formula: t_r = 0.8473 * R_pullup * C_bus (RC time constant to 70% VDD)
equations.verification_source: I2C rise time: t_r = 0.8473 * R * C (ln(0.7^-1) * RC = 0.3567*RC to 70% VDD threshold); NXP I2C-bus spec §3.1; confirmed correct coefficient [Re-confirmed 2026-04-16 post-analyzer-refactor; renumbered from collision with EQ-089 in emc_formulas.py]
